summary: Give clamiga's auto-established per-thread top-level ABORT restart a human-readable :report ("Return to top level") so it no longer prints as "#<RESTART ABORT>" and so tools that princ it / derive an abort reason from it get a description.

items.title: Give the auto-established top-level ABORT restart a :report
items.description:
ROOT CAUSE: src/core/builtins_thread.c thread_entry() establishes a per-thread top-level ABORT restart via `cl_make_restart(SYM_ABORT, abort_handler, CL_NIL, CL_NIL, CL_NIL, abort_tag)` at ~line 162 — the 3rd argument (report) is CL_NIL. The printer's TYPE_RESTART case (src/core/printer.c:1224-1271) only emits a description for PRINC / ~A when r->report is non-NIL; with a NIL report it falls back to the escaped "#<RESTART NAME>" form. That fallback is INTENTIONAL for user-defined restarts without a :report (regression-tested at tests/test_condition.c:666 lisp_restart_no_report_princ) — so DO NOT change the printer. The only defect is that the built-in ABORT restart carries no report.

FIX (src/core/builtins_thread.c): supply a report string to cl_make_restart at ~line 162. Mirror the existing cached lazy-init helper get_thread_abort_handler() (builtins_thread.c:41-52): add `static CL_Obj thread_abort_report = CL_NIL;` and a `static CL_Obj get_thread_abort_report(void)` that on first call does `thread_abort_report = cl_make_string("Return to top level", 19); cl_gc_register_root(&thread_abort_report);` and returns it. Use the exact wording "Return to top level" to match clamiga's own interactive debugger (src/core/debugger.c:130). Pass get_thread_abort_report() as the `report` argument (replacing the first CL_NIL) in the cl_make_restart call. cl_make_restart already GC-protects its report parameter during allocation (src/core/mem.c:535), and the cached string is registered as a GC root, so no extra protection is needed at the call site. The single cached string is shared (read-only) by every worker thread's ABORT restart — safe because strings are immutable here and the printer only reads it.

HOST REGRESSION TEST (tests/test_threads.c): a worker thread sees its top-level ABORT restart, so assert the printed description is now the report rather than the escaped form. Add a test using the eval-string harness already used in that file:
  (mp:join-thread (mp:make-thread (lambda () (princ-to-string (find-restart 'abort)))))  =>  "Return to top level"
Also assert the escaped form is UNCHANGED so the printer's fallback for report-less restarts is not regressed:
  (mp:join-thread (mp:make-thread (lambda () (prin1-to-string (find-restart 'abort)))))  =>  "#<RESTART ABORT>"
If find-restart inside the worker does not resolve the thread-top restart (e.g. due to cl_restart_floor), use (princ-to-string (car (last (compute-restarts)))) instead — the thread-top ABORT restart is the outermost binding. Before the fix the princ assertion returns "#<RESTART ABORT>"; after, "Return to top level".

AMIGA REGRESSION TEST (tests/amiga/run-tests.lisp): add the same two assertions following the existing restart/condition test conventions in that file.

BUG 2 NOTE: throw-to-toplevel returning (:abort "NIL") is produced by SLY's slynk, which is NOT in this repo (loaded externally via quicklisp at runtime). slynk lists SLDB restarts and derives the abort reason from this same auto-established ABORT restart; giving it a proper report is the clamiga-side root-cause fix. Full confirmation that the (:abort ...) reason changes must be done by running ./build/host/clamiga under SLY (a verification step outside clamiga's host/Amiga test suites) — see analysis.json risks.
